headline: Multi-chain yield protocol; no deployed contract addresses catalogued

findings.text: Block explorer name-tag searches not performed; contract addresses not discovered via etherscan.io, basescan.org, arbiscan.io, or other chain explorers.
Official deployments documentation referenced in docs.multipli.fi structure but the Contract Suite page (likely containing addresses) was blocked from fetch.
No audit links recorded in per-protocol context; no audit PDFs to scan for in-scope contract addresses.
No GitHub repository recorded in per-protocol context; cannot walk source for Foundry broadcast artifacts or hardhat-deploy files.
Multi-chain enumeration blocked: protocol.chains lists Ethereum, Base, Monad, Arbitrum, Binance, Avalanche, but no addresses were discovered from any chain's sources.
No factory contract addresses were catalogued; factory enumeration depends on addresses from D1–D5.
Role taxonomy not applicable; no addresses discovered to classify.
Ratchet output integrity: zero addresses in admin_addresses[] due to inability to surface addresses from fetched/blocked sources.

rationale.verdict: Unable to catalogue deployed contract addresses. Website and documentation confirm multi-chain presence and mention rwaUSD, xTokens, and Multipli v2, but do not provide accessible address registries. Contract Suite documentation page blocked from fetch. No GitHub or audit records. Block explorer searches not conducted (required search tool reallocation; used remaining calls on docs discovery). Zero addresses discovered and catalogued.

evidence.shows: App landing page confirms Multipli is a yield infrastructure platform managing $100M+ in assets, supporting multiple chains. No deployed addresses visible on frontend landing.

Main docs site with navigation structure referencing Contract Suite, Collateral and Oracle Profile, User Lifecycle. rwaUSD described as credit-backed stablecoin. No address tables accessible in fetched content.

FAQs confirm protocol operates multi-chain, supports USDT, USDC, BTC, ETH, SOL. Multipli v2 references 4626 vault on Avalanche. Mentions xUSDT, xBTC liquid staking tokens. No contract addresses listed.
Confirms Multipli v2 is built on ERC-4626 vault, currently supports USDC on Avalanche. Plans to add more networks. No addresses provided.

unknowns: D1: Block explorer searches not conducted for each chain; cannot enumerate Multipli contracts via name-tag lookup on etherscan.io, basescan.org, arbiscan.io, optimistic.etherscan.io, polygonscan.com, bscscan.com, snowtrace.io.
D2: Deployed contracts documentation page (likely at https://docs.multipli.fi/technical-overview/contract-suite or similar) blocked from fetch; cannot extract address table.
D3-offchain: No audit links recorded; no audit PDFs available to scan for in-scope address tables.
D4-offchain: No GitHub repository recorded; cannot walk source tree for Foundry broadcast or hardhat-deploy artifacts.
D5: Multi-chain enumeration incomplete; zero addresses confirmed on Ethereum, Base, Monad, Arbitrum, Binance, Avalanche despite confirmed deployments.

protocol_metadata.about: Multipli is a multi-chain yield infrastructure platform designed to aggregate and tokenize real-world assets (RWAs) and cryptocurrencies to generate yield. The protocol abstracts fragmented tokenized assets behind rwaUSD, a unified stablecoin backed by 100+ Treasury-backed stablecoins and 10+ tokenized gold assets. Multipli v2 introduces ERC-4626 vaults for more liquid yield access.
